summary: Front NG is essentially at fair value ($2.995 vs $2.917 fair, +$0.078 spread, status FAIR), so there is no clean mispricing edge right now. The fundamental backdrop leans soft: storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average and price already sits -12.1% YTD vs a -3.4% seasonal median, meaning a lot of bearishness is already priced in. Technicals show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe from 15m to 1w, but the 1h/4h/1d RSI readings (31.2/24.9/37.9) are oversold and daily price is already below its lower Bollinger band, arguing against chasing shorts here. COT shows managed money net short but only at the 31st percentile of the last 5 years, a crowded-but-not-extreme short that carries squeeze risk. Two priority inputs are missing entirely this cycle (near-term weather demand and the +7D/+14D/+21D fair-value projections), which removes the clearest levers for a high-conviction directional call in either horizon.

longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus (+184 Bcf vs 5y) and bearish daily/weekly trend argue for downside, but seasonality reliability is flagged Low, the historically strongest month (Sep) falls inside this window, and COT managed money is already net short at only the 31st percentile (limited room to add before squeeze risk rises). Signals are genuinely mixed with no forward fair-value data to arbitrate.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for the +7D/+14D/+21D fair-value projections to populate and for price to close decisively outside the $2.417-$3.417 band - a close below ~$2.70 would confirm the storage-surplus/bearish-trend thesis, a close above ~$3.25 with COT covering would confirm a squeeze-driven long.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is already FAIR vs the model (front spread only +$0.078) so there is no near-term value edge, and demand_1_7D/demand_8_16D weather reads are both null, removing the primary short-horizon catalyst. Intraday technicals are internally conflicted - 5m is overbought (RSI 67.8, above upper Bollinger) while 1h/4h are oversold (RSI 31.2/24.9) - consistent with a choppy, directionless tape rather than a clean setup.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for weather demand_1_7D/demand_8_16D data to populate and for the daily RSI (37.9) to either reclaim the 50 line with price back above the lower Bollinger band (favors a long toward $3.05-3.10), or for price to break and hold below ~$2.85 (favors a short toward the $2.70-2.60 area).
